findings.summary: hook emitted bare prose to stdout; the harness requires {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:'PreToolUse',additionalContext}} (verified by direct execution + src/cli.ts:702-710 + the live graphify hook-guard), so the Brake was INERT

selftest asserted only non-empty stdout, orthogonal to whether the harness surfaces it — which is how F1 passed three watched mutations; it now parses and asserts the hookSpecificOutput shape, and is watched failing on a bare-prose mutation

false positives undercutting the hook's own anti-desensitisation goal: gh pr review --approve / gh pr edit --add-label, commands quoted or commented, unanchored reviews/ matching src/reviews/, and vendored node_modules/**/docs/adr/
UNVERIFIED by the reviewer: if stdin never closes the 'end' handler never fires and the hook could stall until the harness hook timeout rather than degrading to allow; the timeout was not measured
